text:
SPRINGFIELD, Mo. (KY3) - Inside the Plaza Towers, on the third floor, is an office with quite the proclamation from 28 years ago.

“I was very proud of being part of something that is quite unique,” 88-year-old Irwin Cohen said.

Cohen now consults companies on transportation issues. He worked for close to 40 years with New York’s Transit Authority. He knew all about elevated rail or trains in tunnels. That lead him to Atlanta in the mid-90s, where he was the Director of Car Transportation for the Summer Olympics.

“Clearly I understood the impact of moving people,” Irwin said, “but the Olympics turned my head.”

Four years before the Olympic Games in Atlanta, Irwin was hired to help plan the logistics in Georgia. His experience, paired with his calm demeanor, made him the perfect person to put in charge of the MARTA rail system. The trains would have to be prepared to move a million fans a day.

“We took out seats (from the train cars); it’s like moving cattle,” Irwin said. “So that car is resting on the truck. It’s living on the springs. (There’s) no cushioning, none.”

From the cars themselves to the personnel, Irwin was the lead of moving fans and Atlantans. He also had the idea of putting technicians at the stations so if an issue happened, it could be fixed quickly.

“We had a derailment during the Olympics and I needed that like a hole in the head,” Irwin said.

But thanks to his planning, the train was back on track in an hour and 15 minutes. Irwin had the city’s rail moving like a well-oiled machine.

Irwin was a go-to voice during those Olympic Games. Four years of preparation for two weeks of competition.

“The only thing I could remember is the opening,” Irwin said, “and you know why? Muhammad Ali. I never forgot that. That was only thing that just made me cry.”

“I didn’t see a darn thing other than Muhammad Ali, people, and the trains. So, I missed a lot. But I was happy to be part of that, because if you’re part of the Olympics, it never goes away.”

title: Ozarks Life: Springfieldian planned logistics for the 1996 Olympics
description: Four years before the Olympic Games in Atlanta, Irwin was hired to help plan the logistics in Georgia.
